title: Paper Title
content: Application of surface photovoltage technique in photocatalysis studies on modified TiO2 photo-catalysts for photo-reduction of CO2

title: Abstract
content: Surface photovoltage spectrum (SPS) is a useful technique in photocatalysis studies.Here, the relationship between the catalytic activities of modified TiO 2 , which are composed of PdRuO 2 TiO 2 (A), PdTiO 2 (B), a-TiO 2 (C) and b-TiO 2 (D) for photoreduction of CO 2 and surface photovoltage (SPV) response was studied by means of this technique.The SPV response increased after surface noble metal (Pd and Ru) depositing and a new SPV response band appeared in the SPS curve of A and B in near IR, which was attributed to the surface state population transition formed by Pd deposition.Corresponding to SPV response, the catalytic activity of photoreduction of CO 2 improved after surface noble metal (Pd and Ru) depositing.Moreover, the stronger SPV response is, the higher the catalytic activity of photoreduction of CO 2 is.The effect of metal depositing on the behavior of photo-generated carriers in TiO 2 semiconductor photocatalysts, as well as the mechanism on photoreduction CO 2 of A was discussed.

title: Experimental
content: In this work, four kinds of photocatalysts are PdRuO 2 TiO 2 (A), PdTiO 2 (B), a-TiO 2 (C) and b-TiO 2 (D) powder, and samples A, B and C were prepared as follows the reference 7,8 except for sample D, which was purchased from Fluka.The average particle sizes of samples A, B and C are 22, 19 and 27 nm, respectively. Sample powder (20 mg) was dispersed in aqueous solution containing 0.05 M NaOH and 0.05 M Na 2 SO 3 as an electron donor.Illuminations were performed with a 450 W Xe short arc lamp.The solution was contained in a serum stoppered 30 ml Pyrex glass cuvette and stirred continuously by a magnetic stirring bar, then bubbled CO 2 for 350 mlmin.The quantum yields for the formation of formate was determined with the knowledge of the amount of light absorbed by the system 20. The measurements of SPS were carried out with a solid junction photovoltaic cell (ITOsampleITO) using light source-monochromator-lock-in detection technique.The principle and set-up diagram of SPS measurement were described in detail elsewhere 21.The measurement was performed in air atmosphere and room temperature. Fig. 1 shows the SPS of A, B, C and D. It is obvious that each sample gives a pronounced surface photovoltage (SPV) response band in the range of 300-375 nm (P1), which is assigned to band-band transition of TiO 2 .The order of SPV response intensity is A SPV B SPV C SPV D SPV .It can be known that samples A and B take on higher photo-generated charge separation efficiency.In addition, a negative response band in the range of 350-400 nm appears in the SPS curve of sample D. It can be attributed to the difference in transition character of TiO 2 22.Compared to the SPS of samples C and D, a new SPV response band in the range of 800-1000 nm (P2) appears in the SPS curve of sample A or B, which can be attributed to the sub-band-gap transition of photocatalysts.It can be seen that the surface state was introduced into the surface of TiO 2 , due to noble metal (Pd or Ru) deposition.Additionally, the SPV response intensity of P2 is much weaken than that of P1. Ozkan et al. 23 reported that "little island" of metal particle was existed on the surface of photocatalysts when the noble metal was deposited on the photocatalys surface.These "little island" were the active sites of the photocatalytic reaction, which can absorb reactant and product.They can also transfer the photogenerated electron and hole and avoid the recombination of the light-induced holes and electrons.X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy showed that the Pd adsorbed on the TiO 2 surface was in the 0 and 1 valence states (Pd 0 Pd 1 8), while the Ru was in the 4 valence states and a little 3 valence states.The Pd 1 is assumed to arise from the presence of surface Pd 2 O, and Ru 3 is Ru 2 O 3 .So, we suggested that the depopulation surface state would be introduced into the surface of samples A and B, duo to Pd 0 (or Pd 1 ) adsorbing on the TiO 2 surface, while the population surface state would be formed due to RuO 2 adsorbing on the A surface. Gatos et al. 14 suggested that introduction of depopulation surface state in a semiconductor surface will cause a striking enhancement of the surface photovoltage in the region of intrinsic excitation.The photo-generated excess carriers suppress the surface barrier through redistribution of electrons and holes in the space charge region.The magnitude of this photovoltage can be as high as the value of the surface barrier, i.e. it can readily exceed the Dember-type surface photovoltage by two orders of magnitude.Moreover, introduction of depopulation surface state in a semiconductor surface can induce photoeffects in an new spectral region, i.e. the presence of surface states will also lead to photovoltage in the longer-wave spectral region.We suggested that the electron of TiO 2 is excited from the valence band into the conduction band under a band-gap light illumination, and the photo-generated electrons move in the conduction band and photo-generated holes diffuse in the valance band.If the surface state is introduced into the TiO 2 surface, the photogenerated electron could inject into the depopulation surface state, and the photogenerated hole could inject into the population surface state, consequently the separation between photogenerated electrons and holes is enhanced.The SPV response of P1 comes from separation of the photogenerated electron-hole pairs from a band-to-band transition under a built-in-field.The more the separation efficient of photogenerated carriers is, the stronger the SPV response is.This is the reason that the Pd (or Ru) deposited photocatalysts (A or B) show a more pronounced P1.Moreover, the electron of TiO 2 can also be excited from the valence band into the depopulation surface state or from the population surface state into the conduction band under a sub-band-gap light illumination, which can induce pronounced SPV response.Here, P2 is induced by electron excited from the valence band into the depopulation surface state. In order to explain the transition property of P2, we investigated their optimal phase of SPS (OP SPV ).Usually, we adopt lock-in amplifier technology in the SPS measurement, thus it is very important to choose the phase.What is called optimal phase (OP SPV ) is that photovoltaic response absolute value is the biggest at this phase (usually it is the biggest positive value).If other measurement conditions do not alter, OP SPV is a eigen-physical quantum which imply much micro-information of photo-generated charge.The OP SPV of P1 is 210 and that of P2 is 40 , while the difference of OP SPV of two transition band is nearly 180 .According to the meaning of phase, photovoltage sign of P1 and P2 is just opposite.If Vs of P1 are positive (or negative), Vs of P2 are negative (or positive), i.e.P1 takes on n (p)-type characteristics, while P2 takes on p (n)-type characteristics.For an n-type semiconductor, the Vs of TiO 2 are negative, i.e. Vs of P1 are negative, which indicate that Vs of P2 are positive.According to the SPS principle, the surface state population transition can induce positive Vs in n-type semiconductor 15, such as TiO 2 .The results clearly show that P2 is a surface state population transition (as shown in Fig. 2).Due to a little photon capture cross-section of surface state population transition (or surface state depopulation transition), the SPV response intensity of P2 is much weaker than that of P1. From the results of photovoltaic properties of A, B, C and D, we can conclude that photocatalysts A and B take on higher photocatalytic activities for reduction of CO 2 . Table 1 shows dependence of photocatalytic activity on SPV response intensities (the peak unit area of P1 or P2 in SPS curve) of A, B, C and D. The stronger the SPV response intensities of photocatalysts is, the higher the yield of product (formate) for photoreduction of CO 2 is; moreover, the photocatalysts whose SPS curves appear P2 show higher photocatalytic activity, i.e. photocatalysts A and B show the higher activity of photoreduction of CO 2 .The results reveal the enhanced photocatalytic properties of A and B powders that are deposited by Pd or Ru on TiO 2 surface.The improved photocatalytic activities of A or B are attributed to effective photogenerated charge separation and accelerating charge transfer of interface by the "little island".Hirano et al. 24 reported that TiO 2 alone is an inefficient catalyst for the photochemical reduction of CO 2 , because the photogenerated electron moves towards bulk.When noble metal (Pd or Ru) deposited onto the TiO 2 surface, metal particles not only offer reaction sites for CO 2 with an excited electron but also perform the reducing species reacting with positive holes on the semiconductor site.In our photoreaction system, the photogenerated electron tunneling from conduction band of TiO 2 to the Pd catalyst site effects CO 2 -HCO 3 -fixation or H 2 -evolution, and the photogenerated hole diffuses to RuO 2 catalyst site effects SO 3 2-.The separation efficiency of photogenerated charge was improved greatly, which lead to much higher photocatalytic activities.The Pd deposited TiO 2 (A and B) has selectivity to formate, because hydrogen atoms or hydrides bind tightly to Pd, disfavoring H 2 production. The mechanism of CO 2 reduction on Pd has been the subject of several reports 252627.Here, are the steps in photocatalytic reaction on photocatalyst A: PdRuO 2 TiO 2 h e --h e - h Pd e - Pd - Pd The absorption of a photon by the photocatalyst A formed an exciton which dissociated to a photoelectron and a photohole (Eq.( 1)).Photoelectrons were trapped at Pd (Eq.( 2)), whereas photoholes were trapped at RuO 2 SO 3 2-(Eq.( 3)), the Pd -converted protons into hydrogen atoms, and then formed Pd-H 2 intermediate (Eq.( 4)), at last, bicarbonate was activated on specific Pd sites followed by Pd-H 2 to formate (HCO 2 -) (Eq.( 5)).The representation of the photoreaction process we propose is shown in Fig. 3. The SPV response increased after surface noble metal (Pd or Ru) depositing and a new SPV response band appeared in the SPS curve of PdRuO 2 TiO 2 or PdTiO 2 in near IR.The catalytic activity of photoreduction of CO 2 improved after surface noble metal depositing.The behavior of photo-generated carriers in noble metal (Pd or Ru) depositing TiO 2 semiconductor photocatalysts was that the photogenerated electron transfer from TiO 2 to Pd surface site promotes the reduction of CO 2 to formate, and photogenerated hole inject into RuO 2 surface site promotes the oxidation of SO 3 2-to SO 4 2-. Fig. 1.The SPS of A, B, C and D in UV (I) and near IR (II) region: (a) PdRuO 2 TiO 2 ; (b) PdTiO 2 ; (c) a-TiO 2 ; (d) b-TiO 2 . Fig. 2. Schematic representations of energy band modes of an n-type semiconductor with a Schottky-type barrier: (a) band-band transition; (b) surface state population transition.Vs and Vs , surface potential difference; CB, conduction band; VB, valence band; Et, surface state level; E F , Fermi level. Fig. 3.The schematic illustration of PdRuO 2 TiO 2 photoreduction of CO 2 . et al. reported application of surface photovoltage technique in catalysis studies 0254-058401 -see front matter 2001 Elsevier Science B.V. All rights reserved. Photovoltaic responses of A, B, C and D and yield of product (formate) a a Here, the SPV is the peak unit area of P1 or P2 in SPS curve. This research was supported by the National Science Foundation of China and Jilin Province Science Committee.

title: Results and discussion
content: Fig. 1 shows the SPS of A, B, C and D. It is obvious that each sample gives a pronounced surface photovoltage (SPV) response band in the range of 300-375 nm (P1), which is assigned to band-band transition of TiO 2 .The order of SPV response intensity is A SPV B SPV C SPV D SPV .It can be known that samples A and B take on higher photo-generated charge separation efficiency.In addition, a negative response band in the range of 350-400 nm appears in the SPS curve of sample D. It can be attributed to the difference in transition character of TiO 2 22.Compared to the SPS of samples C and D, a new SPV response band in the range of 800-1000 nm (P2) appears in the SPS curve of sample A or B, which can be attributed to the sub-band-gap transition of photocatalysts.It can be seen that the surface state was introduced into the surface of TiO 2 , due to noble metal (Pd or Ru) deposition.Additionally, the SPV response intensity of P2 is much weaken than that of P1. Ozkan et al. 23 reported that "little island" of metal particle was existed on the surface of photocatalysts when the noble metal was deposited on the photocatalys surface.These "little island" were the active sites of the photocatalytic reaction, which can absorb reactant and product.They can also transfer the photogenerated electron and hole and avoid the recombination of the light-induced holes and electrons.X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy showed that the Pd adsorbed on the TiO 2 surface was in the 0 and 1 valence states (Pd 0 Pd 1 8), while the Ru was in the 4 valence states and a little 3 valence states.The Pd 1 is assumed to arise from the presence of surface Pd 2 O, and Ru 3 is Ru 2 O 3 .So, we suggested that the depopulation surface state would be introduced into the surface of samples A and B, duo to Pd 0 (or Pd 1 ) adsorbing on the TiO 2 surface, while the population surface state would be formed due to RuO 2 adsorbing on the A surface. Gatos et al. 14 suggested that introduction of depopulation surface state in a semiconductor surface will cause a striking enhancement of the surface photovoltage in the region of intrinsic excitation.The photo-generated excess carriers suppress the surface barrier through redistribution of electrons and holes in the space charge region.The magnitude of this photovoltage can be as high as the value of the surface barrier, i.e. it can readily exceed the Dember-type surface photovoltage by two orders of magnitude.Moreover, introduction of depopulation surface state in a semiconductor surface can induce photoeffects in an new spectral region, i.e. the presence of surface states will also lead to photovoltage in the longer-wave spectral region.We suggested that the electron of TiO 2 is excited from the valence band into the conduction band under a band-gap light illumination, and the photo-generated electrons move in the conduction band and photo-generated holes diffuse in the valance band.If the surface state is introduced into the TiO 2 surface, the photogenerated electron could inject into the depopulation surface state, and the photogenerated hole could inject into the population surface state, consequently the separation between photogenerated electrons and holes is enhanced.The SPV response of P1 comes from separation of the photogenerated electron-hole pairs from a band-to-band transition under a built-in-field.The more the separation efficient of photogenerated carriers is, the stronger the SPV response is.This is the reason that the Pd (or Ru) deposited photocatalysts (A or B) show a more pronounced P1.Moreover, the electron of TiO 2 can also be excited from the valence band into the depopulation surface state or from the population surface state into the conduction band under a sub-band-gap light illumination, which can induce pronounced SPV response.Here, P2 is induced by electron excited from the valence band into the depopulation surface state. In order to explain the transition property of P2, we investigated their optimal phase of SPS (OP SPV ).Usually, we adopt lock-in amplifier technology in the SPS measurement, thus it is very important to choose the phase.What is called optimal phase (OP SPV ) is that photovoltaic response absolute value is the biggest at this phase (usually it is the biggest positive value).If other measurement conditions do not alter, OP SPV is a eigen-physical quantum which imply much micro-information of photo-generated charge.The OP SPV of P1 is 210 and that of P2 is 40 , while the difference of OP SPV of two transition band is nearly 180 .According to the meaning of phase, photovoltage sign of P1 and P2 is just opposite.If Vs of P1 are positive (or negative), Vs of P2 are negative (or positive), i.e.P1 takes on n (p)-type characteristics, while P2 takes on p (n)-type characteristics.For an n-type semiconductor, the Vs of TiO 2 are negative, i.e. Vs of P1 are negative, which indicate that Vs of P2 are positive.According to the SPS principle, the surface state population transition can induce positive Vs in n-type semiconductor 15, such as TiO 2 .The results clearly show that P2 is a surface state population transition (as shown in Fig. 2).Due to a little photon capture cross-section of surface state population transition (or surface state depopulation transition), the SPV response intensity of P2 is much weaker than that of P1. From the results of photovoltaic properties of A, B, C and D, we can conclude that photocatalysts A and B take on higher photocatalytic activities for reduction of CO 2 . Table 1 shows dependence of photocatalytic activity on SPV response intensities (the peak unit area of P1 or P2 in SPS curve) of A, B, C and D. The stronger the SPV response intensities of photocatalysts is, the higher the yield of product (formate) for photoreduction of CO 2 is; moreover, the photocatalysts whose SPS curves appear P2 show higher photocatalytic activity, i.e. photocatalysts A and B show the higher activity of photoreduction of CO 2 .The results reveal the enhanced photocatalytic properties of A and B powders that are deposited by Pd or Ru on TiO 2 surface.The improved photocatalytic activities of A or B are attributed to effective photogenerated charge separation and accelerating charge transfer of interface by the "little island".Hirano et al. 24 reported that TiO 2 alone is an inefficient catalyst for the photochemical reduction of CO 2 , because the photogenerated electron moves towards bulk.When noble metal (Pd or Ru) deposited onto the TiO 2 surface, metal particles not only offer reaction sites for CO 2 with an excited electron but also perform the reducing species reacting with positive holes on the semiconductor site.In our photoreaction system, the photogenerated electron tunneling from conduction band of TiO 2 to the Pd catalyst site effects CO 2 -HCO 3 -fixation or H 2 -evolution, and the photogenerated hole diffuses to RuO 2 catalyst site effects SO 3 2-.The separation efficiency of photogenerated charge was improved greatly, which lead to much higher photocatalytic activities.The Pd deposited TiO 2 (A and B) has selectivity to formate, because hydrogen atoms or hydrides bind tightly to Pd, disfavoring H 2 production. The mechanism of CO 2 reduction on Pd has been the subject of several reports 252627.Here, are the steps in photocatalytic reaction on photocatalyst A: PdRuO 2 TiO 2 h e --h e - h Pd e - Pd - Pd The absorption of a photon by the photocatalyst A formed an exciton which dissociated to a photoelectron and a photohole (Eq.( 1)).Photoelectrons were trapped at Pd (Eq.( 2)), whereas photoholes were trapped at RuO 2 SO 3 2-(Eq.( 3)), the Pd -converted protons into hydrogen atoms, and then formed Pd-H 2 intermediate (Eq.( 4)), at last, bicarbonate was activated on specific Pd sites followed by Pd-H 2 to formate (HCO 2 -) (Eq.( 5)).The representation of the photoreaction process we propose is shown in Fig. 3.

title: Conclusions
content: The SPV response increased after surface noble metal (Pd or Ru) depositing and a new SPV response band appeared in the SPS curve of PdRuO 2 TiO 2 or PdTiO 2 in near IR.The catalytic activity of photoreduction of CO 2 improved after surface noble metal depositing.The behavior of photo-generated carriers in noble metal (Pd or Ru) depositing TiO 2 semiconductor photocatalysts was that the photogenerated electron transfer from TiO 2 to Pd surface site promotes the reduction of CO 2 to formate, and photogenerated hole inject into RuO 2 surface site promotes the oxidation of SO 3 2-to SO 4 2-. Fig. 1.The SPS of A, B, C and D in UV (I) and near IR (II) region: (a) PdRuO 2 TiO 2 ; (b) PdTiO 2 ; (c) a-TiO 2 ; (d) b-TiO 2 . Fig. 2. Schematic representations of energy band modes of an n-type semiconductor with a Schottky-type barrier: (a) band-band transition; (b) surface state population transition.Vs and Vs , surface potential difference; CB, conduction band; VB, valence band; Et, surface state level; E F , Fermi level. Fig. 3.The schematic illustration of PdRuO 2 TiO 2 photoreduction of CO 2 . et al. reported application of surface photovoltage technique in catalysis studies 0254-058401 -see front matter 2001 Elsevier Science B.V. All rights reserved. Photovoltaic responses of A, B, C and D and yield of product (formate) a a Here, the SPV is the peak unit area of P1 or P2 in SPS curve.
